title: Maximum Subarray Sum (O(n))
description:
Given an array of integers (with possible negatives), find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum and return that sum.

**The point of this problem**: a brute-force O(n³) is easy; an O(n²) version is straightforward; Kadane's algorithm does it in **O(n)** by tracking just two numbers.
hints: Naive: try every (i, j) pair, sum nums[i..j], track max. That's O(n³) (or O(n²) with running sums).
Key insight: at each index i, the best subarray ending here either starts at i, or extends the best ending at i-1.
So you only need ONE running 'best sum ending here' and ONE 'global best'. That's O(n) time, O(1) space.

testCases.description: all negatives — best is the least-negative single element
